codeReview.summary: The feature was supposed to only legalize and commit pre-existing tree residue, but commit a3a89ad bundled substantial new beta behavior including the new /ai-review command processor, note-webhook routing, scope_json persistence, and writer range/suggestion logic. That broad behavioral scope violates the feature contract for tree-state finalization.

issues.description: This feature introduced an entirely new command-processing package with rerun/ignore/resolve/focus behavior instead of only finalizing an existing dirty tree. Shipping ~490 lines of new product logic in a tree-state finalization feature violates the requirement to avoid new behavioral work and makes the handoff non-isolated.

The commit changed webhook runtime behavior by routing note events into command processing and wiring a new command processor into ingress. Tree-state finalization was required to legalize existing residue only; changing live webhook behavior here broadens feature scope beyond finalization.

The commit added new writer behavior for range anchors and suggestion rendering, including policy-dependent suggestion gating. Those are substantive beta-comment-extensions/product changes, not tree cleanup, so this feature does not satisfy its own no-new-behavior contract.

summary: Reviewed the handoff, transcript skeleton, shared-state files, and commit a3a89ad. The feature fails review because the supposed tree-state-finalization commit bundled substantial new beta behavioral work instead of only isolating and legalizing pre-existing dirty-tree residue.
